pattern: A sliding window that expands to satisfy the required character counts and then contracts to drop slack finds the shortest covering substring in one linear pass.
hints: You are hunting the shortest stretch of the text that contains every required character with the right multiplicity, so a window that grows and shrinks fits naturally.
Track how many required characters are still unmet; extend the right edge until the window is valid, then pull in the left edge while it stays valid to minimize length.
Keep a frequency need-map and a satisfied counter; each time the window becomes valid record it if it beats the best so far, and on ties keep the one that started earlier.

edge_inputs.description: classic window scattered across the text
target longer than text, no window
needs two copies of a character
earliest minimal window when two are equally short
duplicates in target with sparse coverage

stress.description: random text over a small alphabet with a short target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    alpha = string.ascii_lowercase[:5]
    n = max(1, size)
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n))
    tlen = rng.randint(1, 4)
    t = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(tlen))
    return [s, t]
stress.note: small alphabet makes valid windows common; tie-break to earliest start is deterministic
